title: Reducing hallucination in structured outputs via Retrieval-Augmented Generation
problem_statement: Generative AI models, particularly large language models, have a propensity to hallucinate, which challenges real-world adoption. This paper addresses reducing hallucinations in structured outputs for enterprise workflows defined by natural language requirements.

evidence_refs.quote: we devised a system leveraging Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to greatly improve the quality of the structured output

evidence_refs.quote: our proposed system significantly reduces hallucinations in the output and improves the generalization of our LLM in out-of-domain settings

title: Fine Tuning vs. Retrieval Augmented Generation for Less Popular Knowledge
problem_statement: Language models exhibit diminished performance when dealing with less-popular or low-frequency concepts and entities, particularly in domain-specific applications. This paper investigates two approaches—Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and fine-tuning (FT) over synthetic data—to enhance LM performance on low-frequency topics in question answering tasks.

explicit_limitations.name: Fine-tuning resource requirements
explicit_limitations.description: Fine-tuning requires extensive resources, making it costly for enriching LMs with less popular factual knowledge.
evidence_phrases: Fine tuning, while beneficial for small LMs, requires extensive resources.

evidence_refs.quote: Fine tuning, while beneficial for small LMs, requires extensive resources.

core_ideas.name: Stimulus RAG approach
core_ideas.mechanism: A new RAG approach that surpasses fine-tuning effectiveness, eliminating the need for costly data augmentation and fine-tuning steps.
evidence_phrases: we propose the new Stimulus RAG approach that surpasses the effectiveness of fine tuning based approaches, thereby eliminating the need for the costly data augmentation and fine tuning step

evidence_refs.quote: we propose the new Stimulus RAG approach that surpasses the effectiveness of fine tuning based approaches, thereby eliminating the need for the costly data augmentation and fine tuning step

evidence_refs.quote: RAG surpasses FT by a large margin particularly for least popular factual knowledge

experiment_signals.setting: Question answering tasks on low-frequency entities using twelve LMs of varying size and type
metrics: Performance on entities of varying popularity

title: Pre-computed memory or on-the-fly encoding? A hybrid approach to retrieval augmentation makes the most of your compute
problem_statement: Retrieval-augmented language models like Fusion-in-Decoder are powerful but expensive due to encoding many retrieved passages. Pre-encoding a corpus into a memory avoids this cost but suffers a quality penalty because the memory is not conditioned on the current input. The core problem is to reduce compute cost while preserving or improving answer quality.

core_ideas.name: Hybrid pre-compute plus on-the-fly encoding

title: Retrieval-augmented Generation to Improve Math Question-Answering: Trade-offs Between Groundedness and Human Preference
problem_statement: LLM-based math tutoring can produce incorrect or curriculum-misaligned responses. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) can improve quality by grounding responses in vetted textbooks, but designers must balance groundedness against human preference.

explicit_limitations.name: Groundedness–preference trade-off
explicit_limitations.description: Responses that are too grounded in the textbook can reduce human preference, limiting the effectiveness of strict grounding.
evidence_phrases: humans prefer responses generated using RAG, but not when responses are too grounded in the textbook content

title: AI-TA: Towards an Intelligent Question-Answer Teaching Assistant using Open-Source LLMs
problem_statement: Responding to thousands of student questions on online QA platforms each semester has considerable human cost, particularly in computing courses with rapidly growing enrollments. The core technical problem is achieving scalable and intelligent question-answering while ensuring data privacy.
